title: Cyclo at Dusk
imageAlt: A weathered cyclo driver navigates a narrow alley in Hanoi at dusk, the warm amber light casting long shadows across the old quarter's textured walls.
artistStatement: This image captures the quiet resilience of Hanoi's cyclo drivers — figures who carry the weight of a fading tradition through streets rapidly changing around them. Shot at golden hour, I sought to honour their dignity against the encroaching neon of modernity.
curatorNote: Tôn's eye for light transforms a documentary impulse into something elegiac. The compression of time — ancient street, aging rider, dying light — is remarkable for a student in their second year.

title: Monsoon Market
imageAlt: Vendors in conical hats arrange vivid tropical fruits under plastic awnings as monsoon rain streams from the eaves of Ben Thanh Market.
artistStatement: The wet season transforms the market into a theatre of colour and urgency. I waited two hours for this particular arrangement of light, water, and commerce to align.
curatorNote: A strong vertical composition that exploits the portrait orientation to heighten the sense of the rain's weight bearing down on the scene.

title: Lotus Study No. 3
imageAlt: A single pink lotus bloom emerges from dark still water, its reflection perfectly symmetrical in the pre-dawn calm of West Lake.
artistStatement: The lotus is a symbol so familiar in Vietnamese culture that it risks becoming invisible. I wanted to restore its strangeness — to see it as if for the first time — by isolating a single bloom against near-total darkness.
curatorNote: Phuoc's technical command of natural light is exceptional. The near-perfect symmetry creates meditative stillness while the slight imperfection of the reflection keeps it alive.

title: Lantern Festival, Hoi An
imageAlt: Hundreds of silk lanterns in red, yellow, and orange drift over the Thu Bon River during the full moon festival, their light shimmering in the water below.
artistStatement: I've photographed this festival three years running. Each time I search for the image that does justice to the feeling — that particular suspension of ordinary time that the lanterns seem to create.
curatorNote: A confident, mature image from Phuoc's earlier archive. The decision to let the reflection dominate the lower half transforms a celebration into something closer to a dream.

title: Wire City
imageAlt: A dense tangle of electrical and telephone wires fills the frame above a Saigon alley, a chaotic grid silhouetted against a flat white sky.
artistStatement: Ho Chi Minh City's wires are an unofficial map of its growth — unplanned, layered, functional, beautiful in their disorder. I wanted to make a portrait of the city as infrastructure.
curatorNote: The flat sky is a risk that pays off: it transforms the wires into pure graphic form without romantic lighting to soften the critique.

title: Morning Offering
imageAlt: An elderly woman in a white ao dai places incense at a temple altar at dawn, smoke rising in soft spirals through the first light entering the pagoda doorway.
artistStatement: I spent three weeks visiting this pagoda each morning before I felt I had earned the trust to photograph the ritual. The smoke and the light are unrepeatable — this was the only frame.
curatorNote: The patience behind this image is as much the story as the image itself. Phuoc has understood that documentary photography is a relationship, not an extraction.
